title: Build a Counter
prompt: Build a Counter class with: increment() to add one, value() to return the current count (starting at 0), and reset() to set it back to 0.

help.plain: Make an object that holds a number, with methods to add one, read it, and reset it.
help.analogy: A tally counter clicker — click to add, read the window, twist to reset.
help.mnemonic: __init__ sets n=0; increment does n+=1; value returns n.
help.nudge: Where should the count live so every method can see it? (On self.)

details.restate: Define a Counter class. A fresh Counter starts at 0. increment() raises it by 1, value() returns the current number, reset() puts it back to 0.

terms.meaning: a function that belongs to a class and acts on the object (self)
